page: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture with 16 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score and a large-block write bandwidth of 193.78 GB/sec, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded data processing is highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 40.79 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.99 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is moderate, landing in the middle 50%, which reflects the shared CPU allocation model. However, database operations and Redis SET performance remain strong, with Redis handling 63.9 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective alternative for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU execution. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local NVMe storage make it well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing. It is also highly capable for static web serving, where it achieved a throughput of 76.18 GB/sec in testing, as well as general-purpose workloads requiring substantial memory capacity.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM and a local 1920 GB NVMe SSD. While its shared CPU allocation results in average multi-core CPU benchmark performance, its memory subsystem is highly capable, achieving top-tier large-block write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also perform exceptionally well. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local storage, this instance is highly effective for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256g-1920s is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining massive RAM capacity, fast local NVMe storage, and strong data compression performance for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 1920 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory performance
Strong database operations and high-throughput Redis caching
Excellent single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression speeds
Optimized for memory-intensive applications and web serving
